sessions.name: Cardio | Loaded Uphill Hike (20lb)
sessions.objective: Build sustained uphill endurance with progressive pack loading

training.details: Set incline to 12-15%. Hike for 35 minutes at steady Zone 2 pace with 20lb pack. Focus on nose-breathing and consistent foot placement.
training.intensityNote: Zone 2 = conversational pace, nose-breathing. You should be able to speak in full sentences.

training.details: Using a 16-18 inch box and 20lb pack. 3 sets of 15 per leg. Step up with full extension, control the descent.
training.intensityNote: Moderate — focus on controlled movement, not speed

training.details: 25lb per hand. 4 x 100m walks with 60 seconds rest between sets.

sessions.cooldown: 5-minute easy walk, then calf and hip flexor stretches

sessions.name: Strength | Loaded Carries & Leg Endurance (25lb)
sessions.objective: Develop pack carrying capacity and single-leg stability

training.details: Using an 8-inch box. 3 sets of 10 per leg. Slow, controlled descent — 3 seconds down, pause, stand back up.
training.intensityNote: Focus on control, not reps. If your knee collapses inward, reduce height.

training.details: 30lb per hand (farmer carry) for 200m, then 40lb single-side suitcase carry for 100m each hand. Rest 90 seconds. 3 rounds.
training.intensityNote: Moderate to hard — maintain posture, no leaning

training.details: 25lb dumbbell. 4 sets of 12. Full depth — hips below knees. 60 seconds rest between sets.
training.intensityNote: Moderate — a weight that's challenging but lets you finish all sets cleanly

training.details: Front plank 45 seconds, side plank 30 seconds each side. 3 rounds. 30 seconds rest between planks.

sessions.cooldown: Foam roll quads, IT band, and calves. Pigeon pose 60 seconds each side.

sessions.name: Climbing | Scramble Confidence & Balance
sessions.objective: Build comfort on exposed terrain and basic mountaineering movement patterns

training.details: 20 minutes of easy bouldering at 2-3 grades below your limit. Focus on footwork precision and balance, not difficulty.
training.intensityNote: Easy — this is movement practice, not power training

training.details: 4 laps on gym routes at your comfortable level. Focus on smooth, efficient movement and controlled breathing. Rest 2 minutes between laps.
training.intensityNote: Moderate — steady climbing at a comfortable grade

training.details: 5 sets of 20-second hangs on a pull-up bar or jug holds. Rest 40 seconds between sets.
3 sets of 12 push-ups. Then band pull-aparts and I/Y/T shoulder patterns, 10 reps each. Antagonist training to prevent injury.

sessions.cooldown: Forearm stretches, shoulder stretches, 5 minutes easy walking

sessions.name: Flexibility | Hip & Ankle Mobility
sessions.objective: Improve ankle dorsiflexion and hip mobility for crampon walking

training.details: Knee-to-wall ankle stretch. Start at comfortable distance. Hold 30 seconds per side. 3 rounds per leg, trying to increase distance by half an inch each round.
training.intensityNote: Stretch to mild discomfort, never pain

training.details: Hold a full-depth squat with heels flat. Use a doorframe or pole for balance if needed. Hold for 20 seconds, rest 20 seconds. 5 rounds. Total hold time target: 100 seconds.
training.intensityNote: Hold at comfortable depth — build duration before depth

training.details: Low lunge stretch 45 seconds each side. Pigeon pose 60 seconds each side. 90/90 hip switches 10 reps each side. 2 rounds through the sequence.
training.intensityNote: Slow, controlled movements. Breathe through the stretch.

training.details: Side-lying thoracic rotation: 10 reps each side, holding end range for 3 seconds. Then thread-the-needle: 8 reps each side.
Standing calf stretch (straight leg) 30 seconds each side. Bent-knee soleus stretch 30 seconds each side. 3 rounds.

sessions.cooldown: 5 minutes of easy walking or gentle movement

suggestedSkillPractice.description: Practice flat-footing (French technique) on moderate-angle terrain. Focus on keeping all points in contact with the surface and maintaining an upright posture.
suggestedSkillPractice.terrain: Snow slope or steep grassy hillside

suggestedSkillPractice.description: Practice cane position on low-angle terrain and low dagger on moderate slopes. Focus on self-belay technique and comfortable grip transitions.
